title: Turborepo Agent Skill
permitted_uses: Agent workflow guidance for Turborepo package-task orchestration, caching, affected-package execution, and monorepo boundary design.
claims_supported: The skill describes Turborepo as a JavaScript/TypeScript monorepo build system that caches task outputs and runs tasks in parallel based on the dependency graph.
The skill says package tasks should live in package-level package.json files, root package.json scripts should delegate through turbo run, and root task logic should be avoided except rare cases.
The skill recommends writing turbo run, not shorthand turbo commands, in package.json scripts and CI workflows.
The skill describes --affected as the primary way to run changed packages plus dependents.
claims_not_supported: That remote caching should be enabled by default for OpenClinXR.
That every root verification task can or should be moved under Turborepo.
That Turborepo boundary rules replace ArchUnitTS rules for OpenClinXR architecture enforcement.
